cards.front: What is a class in morphological image processing?
cards.back: A morphological class is a set of connected pixels with the same pattern

cards.front: What is a pattern in morphological image processing?
cards.back: A pattern in morphological image processing is a set of connected pixels with a specific shape

cards.front: What is the purpose of dilation in morphological image processing?
cards.back: The purpose of dilation is to spread out or expand the size of a pattern in an image

cards.front: What is the purpose of erosion in morphological image processing?
cards.back: The purpose of erosion is to shrink or reduce the size of a pattern in an image

cards.front: What is homogenization in the context of morphological image processing?
cards.back: Homogenization is a morphological operation that assigns a new value to all pixels in a pattern, based on the value of the center pixel
